_README: TODO: point this at the real database (Supabase Postgres) once the admin board
writes comments. Until then this file IS the source of truth and can be edited
by hand. The script reads only the `comments` array; this _README key is ignored.
The seeded comments below are REAL open questions raised on 2026-07-22 — not
placeholder text. Resolve them or delete them; do not leave them to rot.

comments.author: claude
comments.body: Circuit breaker RED deactivates EVERY portfolio at once. Is that the intended blast radius, or behaviour carried over from the old global kill switch that the portfolio-only refactor missed? This is the one place global behaviour survived.

comments.author: claude
comments.body: Test counts disagree across four sources: CLAUDE.md testing section says 511, CLAUDE.md commands section says 400 backend, the /testing page says 535, and the measured suite is 405 backend. Make the count generated by docs:refresh and delete the hand-written figures.
comments.resolution: Deleted the four conflicting hand-written test counts from CLAUDE.md; canonical count is now generated in stats.md.

comments.author: claude
comments.body: Section 5(b) of PRD-001 needs an honest answer: is decision quality instrumented at all? The trades table records what was decided, but nothing appears to measure whether the decisions were good. If nothing measures it, write 'not instrumented' rather than leaving it blank.
comments.resolution: Answered PRD-001 §5(b) honestly: decision quality is NOT instrumented. Raw material exists; nothing joins decision to outcome. Logged as RISK-007.

comments.author: claude
comments.body: docs/product/roadmap.md and frontend/src/data/roadmap.ts are split as planning-layer vs published-view. Watch for drift: if they diverge on what shipped, the TypeScript wins because it is what the site renders.

comments.author: james
comments.body: Example of a resolved comment — kept so the history shows the shape. Resolved comments never appear in INBOX.md.
comments.resolution: Frontmatter convention adopted with a 13-tag controlled vocabulary.
